DDBrowser.Title: ✨ Dungeondraft Tiles
DDBrowser.DropHint: Drop packs, images, or folders here.
DDBrowser.Name: Name
DDBrowser.Author: Author
DDBrowser.NoTiles: No tiles match the current filters.
DDBrowser.DeleteSelectedAssetsConfirm: Remove {count} selected asset(s) from the library? Image files on disk will not be deleted.
DDBrowser.DeleteAssetConfirm: Remove "{name}" from the asset library? The image file on disk will not be deleted.
DDBrowser.DeletePackConfirm: Remove "{name}" and all of its assets from the library? Image files on disk will not be deleted.
DDBrowser.GMOnlyDelete: Only a GM can remove entries from the library.
DDBrowser.SettingsHint: Configure language, asset path, thumbnails, rotation, and scale.
DDBrowser.RebuildLibraryHint: Scans the asset storage path and rebuilds the library from already uploaded packs and images.
DDBrowser.RebuildLibraryConfirm: Scan "{path}" and rebuild the module library from files found there? Current library metadata in this world will be replaced.
DDBrowser.RebuildLibraryEmpty: No imported packs or assets were found in the asset storage path.
DDBrowser.DeleteModuleTilesHint: Deletes placed scene tiles created by this module in the current world.
DDBrowser.DeleteModuleTilesConfirm: Delete {count} tile(s) created by this module from all scenes in the current world? This can break current scenes that use those tiles.
DDBrowser.NoModuleTiles: No tiles created by this module were found.
DDBrowser.DeleteAllModuleDataHint: Deletes placed module tiles and clears the library. IMPORTANT: if Foundry cannot delete files automatically, DELETE THE ASSET FOLDER MANUALLY.
DDBrowser.DeleteAllModuleDataConfirm: Delete {tiles} placed tile(s), {packs} pack(s), and {assets} asset(s)? Existing scenes can break. IMPORTANT: IF FILES REMAIN, DELETE THE ASSET FOLDER MANUALLY.
DDBrowser.AllModuleDataDeleted: Deleted {tiles} placed tile(s), {packs} pack(s), {assets} asset(s), and {files} file(s) from the module asset folder.
DDBrowser.FileDeleteUnsupported: IMPORTANT: FILES WERE NOT DELETED AUTOMATICALLY. DELETE THE MODULE ASSET FOLDER MANUALLY FROM YOUR FOUNDRY DATA FILES.
DDBrowser.ManualDeleteWarning: IMPORTANT: IF ASSET FILES REMAIN AFTER THIS ACTION, DELETE THE MODULE ASSET FOLDER MANUALLY.
DDBrowser.NoModuleData: No module tiles, packs, or assets were found.
